STAT4911_EDA_Account_B

Srinika Kalavala

2026-05-01

dataset <- read.csv("/Users/srinika/Desktop/OSU/Junior Year/Spring/STAT 4911/Battery Project/battery_dataset_20260327.csv")
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
account_b_data <- dataset[dataset$AccountNumber == "account_b", ]
account_b_data$Month <- as.character(account_b_data$Month)

account_b_data$Datetime_parsed <- as.POSIXct(account_b_data$Datetime,
                                             format = "%m/%d/%y %H:%M")
account_b_data$Hour <- as.integer(format(account_b_data$Datetime_parsed, "%H"))

account_b_data$SettlementPointPrice <- account_b_data$SettlementPointPrice / 1000

account_b_data$Datetime_parsed <- as.POSIXct(account_b_data$Datetime, format = "%m/%d/%y %H:%M")
account_b_data$Hour <- as.integer(format(account_b_data$Datetime_parsed, "%H"))

account_b_data$Season <- "Fall"
account_b_data$Season[account_b_data$Month %in% c("December", "January", "February")] <- "Winter"
account_b_data$Season[account_b_data$Month %in% c("March", "April", "May")]           <- "Spring"
account_b_data$Season[account_b_data$Month %in% c("June", "July", "August")]          <- "Summer"

account_b_data$TimeOfDay <- "Night"
account_b_data$TimeOfDay[account_b_data$Hour >= 5  & account_b_data$Hour < 12] <- "Morning"
account_b_data$TimeOfDay[account_b_data$Hour >= 12 & account_b_data$Hour < 17] <- "Afternoon"
account_b_data$TimeOfDay[account_b_data$Hour >= 17 & account_b_data$Hour < 20] <- "Evening"

results <- aggregate(Quantity ~ Month + Season + TimeOfDay,
                     data = account_b_data,
                     FUN = sum)

month_levels <- c("January", "February", "March", "April", "May", "June",
                  "July", "August", "September", "October", "November", "December")
results$Month <- factor(results$Month, levels = month_levels)
results <- results[order(results$Month), ]

print(results)

## 43    August Summer     Night  47818.79

## 27 September   Fall   Morning  67878.13
## 39 September   Fall     Night  43800.58

## 46  December Winter     Night  80226.16
results_b <- aggregate(Quantity ~ Month + Season + TimeOfDay,
                       data = account_b_data, FUN = sum)

month_levels <- c("January", "February", "March", "April", "May", "June",
                  "July", "August", "September", "October", "November", "December")

results_b$Month <- factor(results_b$Month, levels = month_levels)
results_b$TimeOfDay <- factor(results_b$TimeOfDay,
                               levels = c("Morning", "Afternoon", "Evening", "Night"))
results_b <- results_b[order(results_b$Month), ]
ggplot(results_b, aes(x = Month, y = TimeOfDay, fill = Quantity)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_gradient(
    low = "blue", high = "red",
    labels = scales::comma,
    name = "Total Energy\n(kWh)"
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = month.abb) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = c("Night", "Evening", "Afternoon", "Morning")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 12, angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
    plot.title   = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, color = "gray40"),
    plot.caption  = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray50"),
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 11)
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Account B: Total Hourly Energy Consumption Heatmap (May 2022 – May 2024)",
    subtitle = "Red = high total consumption; Blue = low total consumption",
    x        = "Month (May 2022 – May 2024)",
    y        = "Time of Day",
    caption  = "Aggregated sum of hourly kWh readings per month and time-of-day block"
  )

account_b_data <- dataset[dataset$AccountNumber == "account_b", ]
account_b_data$Month <- factor(as.character(account_b_data$Month),
                               levels = c("January", "February", "March", "April",
                                          "May", "June", "July", "August",
                                          "September", "October", "November", "December"))

account_b_data$Datetime_parsed <- as.POSIXct(account_b_data$Datetime, format = "%m/%d/%y %H:%M")
account_b_data$Hour <- as.integer(format(account_b_data$Datetime_parsed, "%H"))

ggplot(account_b_data, aes(x = Month, y = Quantity)) +
  geom_violin(fill = "lightblue", color = "darkblue", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_boxplot(width = 0.1, fill = "white", outlier.color = "red", outlier.alpha = 0.5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = month.abb) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 12, angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
    plot.title   = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, color = "gray40"),
    plot.caption  = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray50")
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Distribution of Hourly Energy Consumption by Month (Account B)",
    subtitle = "Violin shows full distribution; box shows median and IQR; red dots are outliers",
    x       = "Month (May 2022 – May 2024)",
    y       = "Hourly Quantity (kWh)",
    caption = "Each observation represents one hourly energy reading"
  )

ggplot(account_b_data, aes(x = SettlementPointPrice, y = Quantity)) +
  geom_point(color = "blue", alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", color = "red", se = FALSE) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white"),
    plot.background  = element_rect(fill = "white"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "lightblue"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "lightblue"),
    axis.text.x  = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 12),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
    plot.title    = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, color = "gray40"),
    plot.caption  = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray50")
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Energy Consumption vs. Settlement Price (Account B)",
    subtitle = "Red line shows LOESS trend; each point represents one hourly observation",
    x        = "Settlement Point Price ($/kWh)",
    y        = "Quantity (kWh)",
    caption  = "Note: Price converted from $/MWh to $/kWh"
  )
